title: State of Maharashtra v. Praful B. Desai
summary: Supreme Court held that where accused voluntarily caused death knowing it would likely cause death but without premeditation, it amounts to culpable homicide not amounting to murder under IPC 304.

title: Vishaka v. State of Rajasthan
summary: Landmark case establishing guidelines for prevention of sexual harassment at workplace. Court laid down comprehensive guidelines until legislation was enacted.

title: Sushil Sharma v. State (NCT of Delhi)
summary: Delhi High Court convicted accused for murder of wife. Case involved circumstantial evidence including recovery of victim's body parts. Conviction under IPC 302 upheld.

title: State of Karnataka v. Appa Balu Ingale
summary: Supreme Court held that IPC 506 (criminal intimidation) requires proof that threat was made with intent to alarm or cause person to do any act they are not legally bound to do.

title: Rupan Deol Bajaj v. KPS Gill
summary: Punjab & Haryana High Court convicted IPS officer for outraging modesty of woman at party. Landmark judgment on IPC 354 establishing that even single slap on posterior amounts to outrage of modesty.

title: State of UP v. Ram Babu Misra
summary: Supreme Court clarified distinction between culpable homicide and murder. Court explained that murder is culpable homicide with additional special characteristics mentioned in IPC 300.

title: Pyare Lal Bhargava v. State of Rajasthan
summary: Supreme Court held that to constitute criminal breach of trust under IPC 405, there must be entrustment of property and subsequent dishonest misappropriation or conversion.

title: State of Maharashtra v. Chandraprakash Kewalchand Jain
summary: Supreme Court held that for offense under IPC 420 (cheating), inducement and deception must be proved. Mere breach of contract or failure to pay does not constitute cheating.

title: R.S. Nayak v. A.R. Antulay
summary: Supreme Court explained that for criminal conspiracy under IPC 120B, there must be agreement between two or more persons to do illegal act or legal act by illegal means.

title: Navjot Sandhu v. State (NCT of Delhi)
summary: Supreme Court in Parliament Attack case held that conspiracy can be proved by circumstantial evidence. Direct evidence of agreement not always necessary for IPC 120B.

title: Bhupinder Sharma v. State of Himachal Pradesh
summary: Supreme Court held that for IPC 498A (cruelty), harassment must be with view to coerce woman or her relatives to meet unlawful demand for property. Mere cruelty without dowry angle not covered.

title: State of Karnataka v. Selvi
summary: Supreme Court held that narco-analysis, polygraph and brain-mapping tests cannot be forcibly administered. Involuntary administration violates right against self-incrimination under Article 20(3).

title: Sharad Birdhichand Sarda v. State of Maharashtra
summary: Supreme Court laid down principles for conviction based on circumstantial evidence. Held that chain of circumstances must be complete and point only to guilt of accused.

title: Zahira Habibullah Sheikh v. State of Gujarat
summary: Best Bakery case - Supreme Court on witness protection and fair trial. Court held that justice must not only be done but seen to be done. Retrial ordered.

title: Arnesh Kumar v. State of Bihar
summary: Supreme Court issued guidelines on arrest under IPC 498A and other offenses punishable with less than 7 years. Police must justify necessity of arrest.
